steps.title: Simplify the given expression
steps.content: Since $y \gt 0$, $\sqrt{y^{12}} = y^6$. Substitute and simplify: $\frac{4x^{16}y^7}{7x^3 y^6} = \frac{4x^{13}y}{7}$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — I only: Form I is $\frac{x^{13/2}y^{7/2}}{4}$. This has exponent $\frac{13}{2}$ on $x$ and $\frac{7}{2}$ on $y$, and a denominator of 4 — none of these match $\frac{4x^{13}y}{7}$. Form I is not equivalent. ✗
  • Choice B — II only: Form II is $\frac{4x^{10}y^2}{7}$. The exponents $x^{10}$ and $y^2$ do not match $x^{13}$ and $y^1$ in the simplified expression. Form II is not equivalent. ✗
  • Choice C — I and II: Since neither Form I nor Form II equals $\frac{4x^{13}y}{7}$, both cannot be correct. ✗
  • Choice D — Neither I nor II: Neither form is equivalent to the simplified expression $\frac{4x^{13}y}{7}$. ✓
That is the correct answer.
